SuperAdmin.title: SuperAdmin
SuperAdmin.description: Represents a super administrator account with elevated privileges to manage the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SuperAdmin entity.

email.description: Email address of the super administrator.

firstName.description: First name of the super administrator.

lastName.description: Last name of the super administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the super administrator account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the super administrator account was last updated.

Business.title: Business
Business.description: Represents a business using the platform to manage their Google My Business profile and online presence.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Business entity.

status.description: The current status of the business account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the business was added to the platform.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the business was last updated.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a review received by a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

businessId.description: Reference to Business. (Relationship: Business 1:N Review). The business that received the review.

rating.description: Rating given in the review (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the review was received.

LandingPage.title: LandingPage
LandingPage.description: Represents a landing page created for a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LandingPage entity.

businessId.description: Reference to Business. (Relationship: Business 1:N LandingPage). The business that owns the landing page.

content.description: HTML or markdown content of the landing page.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the landing page was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the landing page was last updated.

SubscriptionPlan.title: SubscriptionPlan
SubscriptionPlan.description: Represents a subscription plan that businesses can subscribe to.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubscriptionPlan entity.

description.description: A short description of the plan.

features.description: A list of features included in the plan.

order.description: The display order of the plan.

isActive.description: Whether the plan is currently active and available for subscription.

isPopular.description: Whether the plan should be highlighted as popular.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the plan was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the plan was last updated.

definition.description: Stores super administrator accounts. Used for authentication and authorization.

params.name: superAdminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the super administrator.

definition.description: Stores business accounts managed by super admins.

params.name: businessId
params.description: The unique identifier of the business.

definition.description: Stores reviews associated with a specific business.

params.name: businessId
params.description: The unique identifier of the business that received the review.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

definition.description: Stores landing pages associated with a specific business.

params.name: businessId
params.description: The unique identifier of the business that owns the landing page.

params.name: landingPageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the landing page.

definition.description: Stores all available subscription plans for the platform.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier of the subscription plan.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore data structure is designed to support the Local Leap application, focusing on a SaaS platform for Google Maps and Google My Business optimization.  The structure emphasizes authorization independence and simplified security rules by adhering to the principles outlined in the prompt.  Specifically, the `superAdmins` collection enables global role assignment. The structure uses path-based ownership and dedicated collections.  Authorization independence is achieved by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules.

*   **SuperAdmins:** Super admins are stored in `/superAdmins/{superAdminId}`. These super admins can manage businesses and view reviews.  The existence of a document in `/superAdmins/{superAdminId}` is sufficient to grant admin privileges (DBAC).
*   **Businesses:** Businesses are stored in `/businesses/{businessId}`.  Each business document contains the `superAdminId` of the super admin that owns the business. This denormalization enables authorization independence, as security rules for business documents can be based directly on the `superAdminId` without needing to `get()` the super admin document.
*   **Reviews:** Reviews are stored in `/businesses/{businessId}/reviews/{reviewId}`.  Each review document contains the `businessId` to which it belongs. Security rules for reviews can leverage the `businessId` to ensure only the super admin associated with the business can access the reviews. This follows a path-based ownership model.
*   **Landing Pages:** Landing pages are stored in `/businesses/{businessId}/landingPages/{landingPageId}`. This structure is similar to that of Reviews and enforces similar security constraints. The `businessId` is present within the landing page document.
*   **Subscription Plans:** Subscription plans are stored in `/subscriptionPlans/{planId}`. These are managed by super admins and are read-only for regular business users. This structure allows for easy retrieval and display of plans on the pricing page.
